Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Category Theory Illustrated – Types 67 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Show HN: TurboQuant for vector search – 2-4 bit compression 64 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Show HN: Apfel – The free AI already on your Mac 583 upvotes, 133 comments
  4. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1699 upvotes, 449 comments
  5. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 59 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a frontpage for personal blogs 521 upvotes, 147 comments
  7. A Recipe for Steganogravy 112 upvotes, 28 comments
  8. iNaturalist 190 upvotes, 55 comments
  9. What we learned building 100 API integrations with OpenCode 76 upvotes, 18 comments
  10. What Category Theory Teaches Us About DataFrames 155 upvotes, 48 comments
  11. You can now run a full Linux operating system inside a 6mb PDF 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 54 upvotes, 15 comments
  13. April 2026 TLDR Setup for Ollama and Gemma 4 26B on a Mac mini 254 upvotes, 102 comments
  14. NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns 282 upvotes, 128 comments
  15. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 160 upvotes, 68 comments
  16. How to Make a Sliding, Self-Locking, and Predator-Proof Chicken Coop Door (2020) 19 upvotes, 4 comments
  17. Tailscale's new macOS home 540 upvotes, 288 comments
  18. Samsung Magician disk utility takes 18 steps and two reboots to uninstall 325 upvotes, 166 comments
  19. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 116 upvotes, 58 comments
  20. Solana Drift Protocol drained of $285M via fake token and governance hijack 57 upvotes, 26 comments
  21. ESP32-S31: Dual-Core RISC-V SoC with Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.4, and Advanced HMI 173 upvotes, 102 comments
  22. If you're running OpenClaw, you probably got hacked in the last week 188 upvotes, 128 comments
  23. TDF ejects its core developers 134 upvotes, 89 comments
  24. Age Verification on Systemd and Flatpak 26 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Async Python Is Secretly Deterministic 18 upvotes, 9 comments
  26. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 56 upvotes, 44 comments
  27. Firm boosts H.264 streaming license fees from $100k up to staggering $4.5M 41 upvotes, 30 comments
  28. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 71 upvotes, 64 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 143 upvotes, 335 comments
  30. Update on the eBay Scam 6 upvotes, 8 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 4 April 2026. Source here.
